THE MASSACHUSETTS VETERANS HOME AT CHELSEA

CCN: 225110 · CHELSEA, MA 02150 · Suffolk County

OverviewRatingsInspectionsDeficienciesOwnership & ChainFacility FeaturesRelatedAll Data

Overview

Address
100 SUMMIT STREET, CHELSEA, MA 02150
Phone
6178845660
Certified beds
154
Avg daily residents
122 (79% of beds filled)
Ownership
Government (state)
Provider type

Medicare/Medicaid since

Setting
Urban
3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 4/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
Resident count too small for reliable rating.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-09-19 5 3 2 20 1 20
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-10-09 10 7 3 40 1 40
Total weighted health score
25.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (24)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

24 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-09-19 2025-11-03
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-09-19 2025-11-03
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-09-19 2025-11-03
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2025-07-29 2025-09-13
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-07-29 2025-09-13
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2024-10-09 2024-11-14
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-10-09 2024-11-14
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-10-09 2024-11-14
0699 Provide care or services that was trauma informed and/or culturally competent. D 2024-10-09 2024-11-14
0710 Obtain a doctor's order to admit a resident and ensure the resident is under a doctor's care. D 2024-10-09 2024-11-14
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. D 2024-10-09 2024-11-14
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2024-10-09 2024-11-14
0557 Honor the resident's right to be treated with respect and dignity and to retain and use personal possessions. D 2024-07-17 2024-09-01
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2024-07-17 2024-09-01
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2024-05-14 2024-06-17
0637 Assess the resident when there is a significant change in condition D 2023-08-03 2023-09-15
0640 Encode each resident’s assessment data and transmit these data to the State within 7 days of assessment. D 2023-08-03 2023-09-15
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2023-08-03 2023-09-15
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2023-08-03 2023-09-15
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2023-08-03 2023-09-15
0790 Provide routine and 24-hour emergency dental care for each resident. D 2023-08-03 2023-09-15
0865 Have a plan that describes the process for conducting QAPI and QAA activities. F 2023-08-03 2023-09-15
0867 Set up an ongoing quality assessment and assurance group to review quality deficiencies and develop corrective plans of action. F 2023-08-03 2023-09-15
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2023-08-03 2023-09-15
